columns.extraction_plan: The total number of participants in the 'Darolutamide + docetaxel + ADT' arm is 651, as reported in the 'Received allocated treatment' row under 'Darolutamide + docetaxel + ADT' in the CONSORT flow diagram (Figure 1). The total number of participants in the 'Placebo + docetaxel + ADT' arm is 651, as reported in the 'Received allocated treatment' row under 'Placebo + docetaxel + ADT' in the CONSORT flow diagram (Figure 1).

The 'METHODS' section on page 1 states: 'Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel.' This indicates two distinct treatment arms. The 'Trial Design' section on page 2 further clarifies: 'Enrolled patients were randomly assigned (1:1) to oral darolutamide 600 mg twice daily or matched placebo, in combination with ADT and docetaxel.' Therefore, there are 2 arms included in the trial.

The 'Treatment Arm 1 Regimen' is 'darolutamide plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel'. This is explicitly stated in the 'METHODS' section of the abstract on page 1: 'Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel.' and further confirmed in the 'INTRODUCTION' section on page 1: 'In ARASENS, patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to double-blind treatment with either darolutamide or placebo, in combination with ADT and docetaxel. The darolutamide group had a significant reduction in the risk of death by 32.5% (HR, 0.68; 95% CI, 0.57 to 0.80; P<.0001) compared with the placebo group.'

columns.column_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1
columns.extraction_plan: The abstract on page 1 states that patients were randomly assigned to 'darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel'. The introduction on page 1 refers to darolutamide as an 'androgen receptor pathway inhibitor'. Therefore, the class of agent for Treatment Arm 1 (darolutamide) is 'androgen receptor pathway inhibitor'.

The experimental arm is reported in the abstract on page 1 as "darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel". The experimental arm is specifically "darolutamide plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel". This is further confirmed in the 'INTRODUCTION' section on page 1 and 'Trial Design' section on page 2, which state "darolutamide or placebo, in combination with ADT and docetaxel". The most consistent and complete name for the experimental arm is "Darolutamide + ADT + docetaxel".

The control arm is 'Placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel'. This is explicitly stated in the 'METHODS' section of the abstract on page 1: 'Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel'.

The 'Study Assessments' section on page 3 mentions 'time to worsening of disease-related physical symptoms as measured by the Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Prostate (National Comprehensive Cancer Network) symptom index 17-item questionnaire', which indicates that quality of life or patient-reported outcomes related to symptoms were assessed.
Extract the value 'NCT02799602' from the 'CLINICAL TRIAL INFORMATION' section on page 12.
The PubMed ID is not explicitly reported in the provided PDF. While there are references, a direct PubMed ID for the main publication is not found.

Extract 'ARASENS' from the title of the paper on page 1: 'Darolutamide Plus Androgen-Deprivation Therapy and Docetaxel in Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer by Disease Volume and Risk Subgroups in the Phase III ARASENS Trial'.

Extract the author list from page 1: 'Maha Hussain, MD¹; Bertrand Tombal, MD, PhD2; Fred Saad, MD³; Karim Fizazi, MD, PhD4; Cora N. Sternberg, MD5; E. David Crawford, MD; Neal Shore, MD7; Evgeny Kopyltsov, MD; Arash Rezazadeh Kalebasty, MD; Martin Bögemann, MD10; Dingwei Ye, MD11; Felipe Cruz, MD, PhD12; Hiroyoshi Suzuki, MD, PhD13; Shivani Kapur, MD14; Shankar Srinivasan, PhD15; Frank Verholen, MD16; Iris Kuss, MD17; Heikki Joensuu, MD18; and Matthew R. Smith, MD, PhD19'.
Extract '2023' from the copyright information at the bottom of page 1: '© 2023 by American Society of Clinical Oncology'.
The document is a multi-page article with detailed methods, results, and discussion sections, indicating it is a 'Full Pub'.

Extract 'Phase III' from the title of the paper on page 1: '...in the Phase III ARASENS Trial'. Also confirmed in the 'Trial Design' section on page 2: 'ARASENS was a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, phase III trial'.

The abstract on page 1 states: 'We examined efficacy and safety from the ARASENS trial for subgroups by disease volume and risk.' and the 'Knowledge Generated' section on page 2 states: 'These subgroup analyses of ARASENS showed...'. This indicates a 'Follow Up' analysis of the ARASENS trial.
The total number of participants included in the clinical trial is found in Figure 1, under the 'Patients randomly assigned' box, which states 'N = 1,306'.

The 'Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Prostate (National Comprehensive Cancer Network) symptom index 17-item questionnaire' is reported as a measure for time to worsening of disease-related physical symptoms. This is a quality of life scale. It is found on page 3, in the 'Study Assessments' section, second paragraph. Additionally, the 'Brief Pain Inventory Short-Form questionnaire' is mentioned for pain progression, which is also a quality of life related measure.

The total number of participants in the control arm is found in Figure 1, 'CONSORT flow diagram'. Locate the 'Patients randomly assigned' section and identify the number (n) next to 'Placebo + docetaxel + ADT'. The value is 655.

The median follow-up duration for the entire study or for the treatment arms is not explicitly reported in the document. The document mentions 'median treatment duration' (page 4, page 11) and 'median survival' (page 4), but not 'median follow-up duration'.
The median treatment duration for darolutamide plus ADT and docetaxel in ARASENS is explicitly stated as '41 months' in the 'DISCUSSION' section on page 11, first paragraph.

The overall median on-treatment duration for the placebo arm in the ARASENS trial is not explicitly reported in the document. While subgroup-specific durations for the placebo arm are mentioned on page 4 (e.g., 15.3 months for high-volume, 26.5 months for low-volume), an overall median value for the control arm is not provided. The text on page 11 refers to a 34.1-month duration for a different trial (PEACE-1), not the ARASENS control arm.

The name of the clinical study is 'ARASENS'. It is explicitly mentioned in the title of the article: 'Darolutamide Plus Androgen-Deprivation Therapy and Docetaxel in Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer by Disease Volume and Risk Subgroups in the Phase III ARASENS Trial'. It is also mentioned multiple times throughout the abstract, introduction, and in the titles of tables and figures (e.g., Table 1 on page 5, Figure 2 on page 6).
The median age for the treatment arm (Darolutamide) is found in Table 1, under the 'All Patients - Darolutamide (n = 651)' column, row 'Age, median, years (range)'. The value is 67.0.
The median age for the control arm (Placebo) is found in Table 1, under the 'All Patients - Placebo (n = 654)' column, row 'Age, median, years (range)'. The value is 67.0.

columns.column_name: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Synchronous
columns.extraction_plan: The 'RESULTS' section on page 1 states: 'Darolutamide increased overall survival (OS) versus placebo in patients with high-volume (hazard ratio [HR], 0.69; 95% CI, 0.57 to 0.82), high-risk (HR, 0.71; 95% CI, 0.58 to 0.86), and low-risk disease (HR, 0.62; 95% CI, 0.42 to 0.90), and in the smaller low-volume subgroup, the results were also suggestive of survival benefit (HR, 0.68; 95% CI, 0.41 to 1.13).' The 'Timing of metastatic presentation and disease volume and risk' paragraph on page 2 mentions 'The presence of metastatic disease at initial diagnosis (de novo or synchronous disease) is associated with poorer outcomes...' and 'In ARASENS, the treatment effect of darolutamide on OS was favorable in patients with de novo (HR, 0.71; 95% CI, 0.59 to 0.85) and recurrent (HR, 0.61; 95% CI, 0.35 to 1.05) metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer.9'. Table A1 on page 18, under 'Metastatic stage at initial diagnosis', includes 'De novo' as a subgroup, which is synonymous with synchronous.

columns.column_name: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Metachronous
columns.extraction_plan: The 'RESULTS' section on page 1 states: 'Darolutamide increased overall survival (OS) versus placebo in patients with high-volume (hazard ratio [HR], 0.69; 95% CI, 0.57 to 0.82), high-risk (HR, 0.71; 95% CI, 0.58 to 0.86), and low-risk disease (HR, 0.62; 95% CI, 0.42 to 0.90), and in the smaller low-volume subgroup, the results were also suggestive of survival benefit (HR, 0.68; 95% CI, 0.41 to 1.13).' The 'Timing of metastatic presentation and disease volume and risk' paragraph on page 2 mentions 'The presence of metastatic disease at initial diagnosis (de novo or synchronous disease) is associated with poorer outcomes compared with developing metastases after local therapy (recurrent or metachronous disease).' and 'In ARASENS, the treatment effect of darolutamide on OS was favorable in patients with de novo (HR, 0.71; 95% CI, 0.59 to 0.85) and recurrent (HR, 0.61; 95% CI, 0.35 to 1.05) metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer.9'. Table A1 on page 18, under 'Metastatic stage at initial diagnosis', includes 'Recurrent' as a subgroup, which is synonymous with metachronous.

columns.column_name: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | High volume
columns.extraction_plan: The 'PURPOSE' section on page 1 states: 'We examined efficacy and safety from the ARASENS trial for subgroups by disease volume and risk.' The 'RESULTS' section on page 1 states: 'Of 1,305 patients, 1,005 (77%) had high-volume disease and 912 (70%) had high-risk disease. Darolutamide increased overall survival (OS) versus placebo in patients with high-volume (hazard ratio [HR], 0.69; 95% CI, 0.57 to 0.82)...'. Figure 2A on page 6 is titled 'OS in subgroups of patients by (A) high-volume disease'. Table 1 on page 5 is titled 'Baseline Demographics and Patient Characteristics for the Subgroups of Patients by Disease Volume and Disease Risk in ARASENS' and includes 'High Volume' as a primary column header. Table 2 on page 8, Table 3 on page 9, Table 4 on page 10, and Table A1 on page 18 all include 'High Volume' subgroups.

columns.column_name: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Low volume
columns.extraction_plan: The 'PURPOSE' section on page 1 states: 'We examined efficacy and safety from the ARASENS trial for subgroups by disease volume and risk.' The 'RESULTS' section on page 1 states: 'Darolutamide increased overall survival (OS) versus placebo in patients with high-volume (hazard ratio [HR], 0.69; 95% CI, 0.57 to 0.82), high-risk (HR, 0.71; 95% CI, 0.58 to 0.86), and low-risk disease (HR, 0.62; 95% CI, 0.42 to 0.90), and in the smaller low-volume subgroup, the results were also suggestive of survival benefit (HR, 0.68; 95% CI, 0.41 to 1.13).'. Figure 2B on page 6 is titled 'OS in subgroups of patients by (B) low-volume disease'. Table 1 on page 5 is titled 'Baseline Demographics and Patient Characteristics for the Subgroups of Patients by Disease Volume and Disease Risk in ARASENS' and includes 'Low Volume' as a primary column header. Table 2 on page 8, Table 3 on page 9, Table 4 on page 10, and Table A1 on page 18 all include 'Low Volume' subgroups.

columns.column_name: Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Treatment
columns.extraction_plan: The number of participants in the Darolutamide treatment arm with high volume disease is 497, found in Table 1 under the 'High Volume - Darolutamide (n = 497)' column. The total number of patients in the Darolutamide arm is 651 (sum of High Volume Darolutamide (n=497) and Low Volume Darolutamide (n=154) from Table 1). The percentage is calculated as (497 / 651) * 100 = 76.3%.

columns.column_name: Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Control
columns.extraction_plan: The number of participants in the Placebo control arm with high volume disease is 508, found in Table 1 under the 'High Volume - Placebo (n = 508)' column. The total number of patients in the Placebo arm is 654 (sum of High Volume Placebo (n=508) and Low Volume Placebo (n=146) from Table 1). The percentage is calculated as (508 / 654) * 100 = 77.7%.

columns.column_name: Volume of disease - N (%) | Low | Control
columns.extraction_plan: The number of participants in the Placebo control arm with low volume disease is 146, found in Table 1 under the 'Low Volume - Placebo (n = 146)' column. The total number of patients in the Placebo arm is 654 (sum of High Volume Placebo (n=508) and Low Volume Placebo (n=146) from Table 1). The percentage is calculated as (146 / 654) * 100 = 22.3%.

The 'Darolutamide + docetaxel + ADT' arm had 651 patients assigned, and 651 patients 'Received allocated treatment'. Since docetaxel was part of the allocated treatment, this means 651 (100%) patients in the treatment arm received docetaxel. This is found in Figure 1, 'CONSORT flow diagram', under the 'Darolutamide + docetaxel + ADT' section.

The 'Placebo + docetaxel + ADT' arm had 655 patients assigned, and 651 patients 'Received allocated treatment'. Since docetaxel was part of the allocated treatment, this means 651 (99.4%) patients in the control arm received docetaxel. This is found in Figure 1, 'CONSORT flow diagram', under the 'Placebo + docetaxel + ADT' section. The percentage is calculated as (651/655)*100 = 99.39%.
The primary endpoint is "Overall Survival (OS)". This is explicitly stated on page 3, under the "Study Assessments" section: "OS was the primary end point."

columns.column_name: Mode of metastases - N (%) | Synchronous | Treatment
columns.extraction_plan: In Table 1, locate the row 'Metastasis stage at initial diagnosis, No. (%)' and the sub-row 'De novo mHSPC'. Sum the counts for 'Darolutamide (n = 497)' under 'High Volume' (432) and 'Darolutamide (n = 154)' under 'Low Volume' (126) to get the total count for the treatment arm (432 + 126 = 558). The total number of patients in the Darolutamide arm is 651 (from Table 2 header 'All Patients - Darolutamide (n = 651)'). Calculate the percentage as (558 / 651) * 100 = 85.71%. The value is 558 (85.71%).

columns.column_name: Mode of metastases - N (%) | Synchronous | Control
columns.extraction_plan: In Table 1, locate the row 'Metastasis stage at initial diagnosis, No. (%)' and the sub-row 'De novo mHSPC'. Sum the counts for 'Placebo (n = 508)' under 'High Volume' (445) and 'Placebo (n = 146)' under 'Low Volume' (121) to get the total count for the control arm (445 + 121 = 566). The total number of patients in the Placebo arm is 654 (from Table 2 header 'All Patients - Placebo (n = 654)'). Calculate the percentage as (566 / 654) * 100 = 86.54%. The value is 566 (86.54%).

columns.column_name: Mode of metastases - N (%) | Metachronous | Treatment

The value is 86 (13.21%).

columns.column_name: Mode of metastases - N (%) | Metachronous | Control
columns.extraction_plan: In Table 1, locate the row 'Metastasis stage at initial diagnosis, No. (%)' and the sub-row 'Recurrent mHSPC'. columns.column_name: Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade 3 or Higher | Treatment
columns.extraction_plan: Sum the number of patients for 'Grade 3', 'Grade 4', and 'Grade 5 (death)' under 'Worst grade' for 'All Patients - Darolutamide (n = 652)' in Table 3. The total is 248 + 183 + 27 = 458 patients. Calculate the percentage: (458 / 652) * 100 = 70.2%. So, 458 (70.2%).

columns.column_name: Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade 3 or Higher | Control
columns.extraction_plan: Sum the number of patients for 'Grade 3', 'Grade 4', and 'Grade 5 (death)' under 'Worst grade' for 'All Patients - Placebo (n = 650)' in Table 3. The total is 232 + 181 + 26 = 439 patients. Calculate the percentage: (439 / 650) * 100 = 67.5%. So, 439 (67.5%).

columns.column_name: Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-related Grade 3 or Higher | Treatment
columns.extraction_plan: Table 3 reports 'Treatment-Emergent AEs' by worst grade, but does not specify if these AEs are 'treatment-related' (i.e., causally linked to the investigational drug, darolutamide). The abstract mentions that 'many were known toxicities related to docetaxel', which was given in both arms, but does not provide a breakdown of Grade 3 or higher AEs specifically related to darolutamide.

columns.extraction_plan: Table 3 reports 'Treatment-Emergent AEs' by worst grade, but does not specify if these AEs are 'treatment-related' (i.e., causally linked to the investigational drug, darolutamide). The abstract mentions that 'many were known toxicities related to docetaxel', which was given in both arms, but does not provide a breakdown of Grade 3 or higher AEs specifically related to the placebo arm's unique components or overall treatment-related AEs.

columns.column_name: Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-related Grade 5 | Treatment
columns.extraction_plan: Table 3 reports 'Grade 5 (death)' for 'All Patients - Darolutamide' as 27 (4.1%). However, footnote 'd' indicates that 'Specific grade 5 events are provided in the supplementary appendix of Smith et al.', and the abstract states that 'Most deaths were related to disease progression (75.1% in the darolutamide group)'. This implies that the Grade 5 events are primarily due to disease progression, not treatment-related. The document does not provide the number or percentage of Grade 5 events that are explicitly treatment-related.

columns.extraction_plan: Table 3 reports 'Grade 5 (death)' for 'All Patients - Placebo' as 26 (4.0%). However, footnote 'd' indicates that 'Specific grade 5 events are provided in the supplementary appendix of Smith et al.', and the abstract states that 'Most deaths were related to disease progression (79.9% in the placebo group)'. This implies that the Grade 5 events are primarily due to disease progression, not treatment-related. The document does not provide the number or percentage of Grade 5 events that are explicitly treatment-related.

The add-on treatments are 'androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT)' and 'docetaxel'. This information is explicitly stated in the abstract and introduction sections. For example, in the abstract: 'Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel.'

The treatment classes are 'Darolutamide + ADT + Docetaxel' and 'Placebo + ADT + Docetaxel'. This information can be found in the abstract's methods section (page 1), the introduction (page 1), and the methods section (page 2) describing the trial design.

The document reports on 'overall survival (OS)' and 'time to castration-resistant prostate cancer' as key efficacy endpoints, but 'Progression-Free Survival (PFS) Rate' for the overall treatment arm is not explicitly reported with hazard ratios and confidence intervals. While 'time to castration-resistant prostate cancer' is a progression-related endpoint, it is not synonymous with 'Progression-Free Survival (PFS)' as defined.

The document reports on 'overall survival (OS)' and 'time to castration-resistant prostate cancer' as key efficacy endpoints, but 'Progression-Free Survival (PFS) Rate' for the overall control arm is not explicitly reported with hazard ratios and confidence intervals. While 'time to castration-resistant prostate cancer' is a progression-related endpoint, it is not synonymous with 'Progression-Free Survival (PFS)' as defined.

The median overall survival for all patients in the experimental arm is 'NE' (Not Estimable), as reported in Table A1, page 16, under the 'Overall' row, 'Darolutamide + ADT + Docetaxel' column, 'Median' sub-column. This is consistent with the text on page 4, which states that median survival was not reached in the darolutamide group for high-volume patients, and Figure 2 (page 6) which shows 'NE' for all darolutamide subgroups.

The median overall survival for all patients in the control arm is 42.4 months, as reported in Table A1, page 16, under the 'Overall' row, 'Placebo + ADT + Docetaxel' column, 'Median' sub-column. This is consistent with the text on page 4, which states 'Median survival was not reached in the darolutamide group and was 42.4 months (95% CI, 39.7 to 46.0 months) in the placebo group'.

The median overall survival for patients with high volume metastasis in the experimental arm is 'NE' (Not Estimable), as reported in Figure 2A, page 6, for 'Darolutamide + ADT + docetaxel Median'. The text on page 4 also states, 'Median survival was not reached in the darolutamide group' for high-volume patients.

The median overall survival for patients with high volume metastasis in the control arm is 42.4 months, as reported in Figure 2A, page 6, for 'Placebo + ADT + docetaxel Median'. The text on page 4 also states, 'Median survival was not reached in the darolutamide group and was 42.4 months (95% CI, 39.7 to 46.0 months) in the placebo group' for high-volume patients.

The median overall survival for patients with low volume metastasis in the experimental arm is 'NE' (Not Estimable), as reported in Figure 2B, page 6, for 'Darolutamide + ADT + docetaxel Median'. The text on page 4 also states, 'Median survival was not reached in either treatment group for low-volume patients'.

The median overall survival for patients with low volume metastasis in the control arm is 'NE' (Not Estimable), as reported in Figure 2B, page 6, for 'Placebo + ADT + docetaxel Median'. The text on page 4 also states, 'Median survival was not reached in either treatment group for low-volume patients'.

The PDF is a report of a single randomized controlled trial (ARASENS), which presents its methods, results, and conclusions. It does not contain an 'overall risk judgment of individual criteria of evidence' for randomized controlled trials, which would typically be found in a systematic review or meta-analysis assessing the quality or risk of bias of multiple studies, or a formal self-assessment using a specific risk of bias tool.

The abstract on page 1 states: "Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel." This describes the two types of therapy provided in the trial.

The value is found in Table 1, under the 'Gleason score at initial diagnosis,d No. (%)' row, sub-row '<8'. To get the total for the 'Treatment' arm (Darolutamide), sum the counts from 'High Volume - Darolutamide (n = 497)' (96) and 'Low Volume - Darolutamide (n = 154)' (26). The total count is 96 + 26 = 122. The total number of patients in the Darolutamide arm is 651. The percentage is (122 / 651) * 100 = 18.74%. Therefore, the value is 122 (18.7%).

The value is found in Table 1, under the 'Gleason score at initial diagnosis,d No. (%)' row, sub-row '<8'. To get the total for the 'Control' arm (Placebo), sum the counts from 'High Volume - Placebo (n = 508)' (87) and 'Low Volume - Placebo (n = 146)' (31). The total count is 87 + 31 = 118. The total number of patients in the Placebo arm is 654. The percentage is (118 / 654) * 100 = 18.04%. Therefore, the value is 118 (18.0%).
